title: Professional squash competition starts today
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The George Squash Club...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The George Squash Club held its first ever Professional Squash Association (PSA) Tour event from 24 to 26 January.It was also the first ever PSA event held in the Southern Cape.

Another PSA Tour event starts today, Thursday 30 January, in Mossel Bay and it concludes on Sunday. (See the list of players and the match schedule below.)

National and international squash players, as well as Eden players, are competing in this event, taking place at the squash courts at the Mossel Bay Golf Estate in Linkside in town.

The Eden players taking part in this competition are:

Jean-Pierre Brits - Mossel Bay (a former Protea player)Christian Swanepoel - GeorgeRoss Smorfitt - GeorgeOz Campbell - KnysnaIan de Jager - GeorgeMikhail Schabort - George


> Last weekend's event drew entries from all over South Africa, as well as from as far afield as Namibia and Zambia.


With squash making its debut as an Olympic sport at the 2028 Olympic and Paralympic Games in Los Angeles (LA28), much effort is being made worldwide to raise the profile of the sport.


> The matches were of a high standard last weekend and the event gave the players the opportunity to gain PSA ranking points.


Results of last weekend's PSA Tour event in George:

PSA men's winner: Damian Groenewald (Pretoria)Runner-up: Luhann Groenewald (Pretoria)PSA women's winner: Helena Hudson (Pretoria)Runner-up: Jordin Phillips (Pretoria)Men's A-section winner: Lukumba Muchimba (Zambia)Runner-up: Oz Campbell (Knysna)Men's B-section winner: Cody Abrahams (Riversdale)Runner-up: Regan-John Daniels (Bellville).

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
